metadata.title: Open-source structured force-capability dataset
metadata.scope: Public/open-source capability fields for requested Blue/Red force systems, augmented with declassified/formerly classified public intelligence context where it can be safely included.

classification_handling.policy: This dataset includes only material that is publicly releasable: official public data, declassified/formerly classified releases, public intelligence assessments, and clearly labelled OSINT estimates. It does not include active classified information, non-public operational data, or leaked classified material.
classification_handling.why_active_classified_not_included: Information remains sensitive when it is still classified, derived from leaks, or would expose current operational readiness, sensor performance, electronic-warfare parameters, target-quality locations, tactics, or vulnerabilities. Those fields are retained as null with explanatory notes rather than filled with unverified or unsafe values.
classification_handling.unknown_values: Fields that are classified, disputed, or not credibly public are set to null with explanatory notes.
classification_handling.pk_values: Probability-of-kill values are usually test-condition-dependent and classified; public marketing claims are marked low confidence when included.
classification_handling.radar_vs_stealth: Radar range against stealth targets is not credibly public and is left null unless a source gives a non-sensitive generic statement.
classification_handling.ew_jam_probability: Jamming probabilities, waveforms, bands, and exact effects are scenario-dependent and generally not credibly public; left null.
corrections: Swedish NH90 service designation is Helikopter 14 (Hkp 14), not Helikopter 15. Helikopter 15 is the AgustaWestland A109 LUH.
RBS 15F is the air-launched Gripen anti-ship missile. Land/coastal RBS 15 systems are usually RBS 15KA or newer land-based RBS15 Mk3/Mk4/Gungnir concepts.
Russian Steregushchiy Project 20380 corvettes normally carry Kh-35 Uran and Redut, not Kalibr; Project 20385 derivatives carry UKSK/Kalibr.

releasability_categories.official_public: Published by a manufacturer, military, government, or international organization for public release.
releasability_categories.formerly_classified_declassified: Previously classified or intelligence-derived material that has been officially declassified/released.
releasability_categories.public_intelligence_assessment: Unclassified public assessment by a government intelligence/defense body.
releasability_categories.osint_estimate: Open-source estimate from defense reference works, research institutes, or reputable reporting; uncertainty must remain explicit.
releasability_categories.media_report_unverified: Public reporting that may be useful for awareness but is not authoritative enough for precision modelling.
releasability_categories.not_included_active_classified_or_leaked: Requested or plausible field is withheld because it is not public, appears derived from leaks, or would be operationally sensitive.

range.note: Approximate ferry/maximum range; mission range varies strongly with tanks, loadout and profile.

combat_radius.note: Common open-source planning estimate; not an official Saab figure and varies by mission profile.

max_speed.note: Mach/true speed varies by altitude and load.

type.value: PS-05/A pulse-Doppler multimode X-band mechanically scanned radar

fighter.note: Frequently cited open-source figure; exact radar performance is classified.

bomber_large_target.note: Estimate scaled from larger RCS targets; not authoritative.

stealth_low_observable.note: No credible public range. Depends on aspect, frequency, RCS, environment and EW.
stealth_low_observable.sensitivity_note: Not credibly public or operationally sensitive; do not infer a value.

Sweden.defensive_area_denial: Layered air, coastal, ground and civil-defence measures to deny use of Swedish territory and littorals.
Sweden.dispersed_basing: Gripen and support units are designed for road-base/dispersed operations with rapid turnaround.
Sweden.shoot_and_scoot: Air defence, artillery and coastal missile units emphasize mobility and survivability.
Sweden.total_defense_concept: Military defence integrated with civil preparedness, continuity of government and societal resilience.

NATO.air_centric_coalition_ops: Air tasking order, AWACS/C2, datalinks and coalition tanker/ISR networks.
NATO.SEAD_DEAD_workflows: Find/fix/track/target/engage/assess cycles using ISR, EW, cyber, decoys, anti-radiation weapons and standoff fires.
NATO.A2AD_penetration_playbooks: Integrated EW, stealth, standoff weapons, decoys, cruise missiles, cyber and kinetic attacks against sensors/C2/shooters.
NATO.caveat: Exact playbooks and weaponeering are classified; this is a generic doctrinal abstraction.

Russia.hybrid_probing: Political, cyber, information and military probes may precede conventional escalation.
Russia.PROBE_FEINT_SATURATION: Generic escalation model: reconnaissance/probing, feints to stimulate sensors, then fires/drones/missiles to saturate.
Russia.EW_integration: EW used to disrupt C2, GNSS, drones and radars; effects are localized and scenario-dependent.
Russia.drone_first_reconnaissance: UAS/artillery kill-chain use emphasizes Orlan-type ISR, Lancet/FPV and counter-battery support.

China.A2AD_layered_defense: Long-range missiles, integrated air defence, maritime strike, space/cyber/EW and naval/air forces to complicate adversary access.
China.long_range_missile_saturation: Rocket Force and naval/air-launched missiles can be used for salvos against bases, ports and naval forces.
China.drone_swarm_tactics: Public doctrine discusses intelligentized/unmanned systems; exact swarm concepts not public.
China.joint_operations_doctrine: The PLA emphasizes joint theater commands, systems confrontation and multi-domain operations.

JAS_39E.value: 3.4 t internal estimate; sortie burn commonly 3-6 t depending profile

F_16.value: 3.2 t internal; sortie burn 3-7 t depending tanks/profile

F_35A.value: 8.3 t internal fuel official RAAF; sortie burn 5-10+ t depending profile

Su_35S.value: 9-11 t internal fuel estimate; sortie burn highly profile-dependent

artillery_155mm.note: Scenario-dependent planning heuristic, not a national doctrine figure.

SAM_interceptors.value: threat-driven; salvo doctrine often 1-2 interceptors per target but varies by system and target

factory_to_national_depot.value: weeks to months for missiles/complex munitions; days to weeks for bulk consumables inside Europe

national_depot_to_forward_depot.value: 1-7 days in permissive rear areas; longer under interdiction

forward_depot_to_base_or_firing_unit.value: 0.5-2 days typical planning factor; minutes-hours for prepositioned stocks

rest.value: 10-12 h protected rest per 24 h target

naval_crews.watch_rotation: 3- or 4-section watch systems reduce fatigue; battle stations cannot be sustained indefinitely.

maintenance_hours_per_flight_hour.caveat: Comparable maintenance metrics are notoriously inconsistent because air forces count personnel, contractor, depot, and fault-isolation hours differently.

forest.notes: Roads dominate mobility; off-road wheeled movement restricted.

wetland_bog.notes: Can canalize movement to roads/causeways; seasonal freeze changes this.

urban.notes: Rubble, obstacles and canalized streets slow vehicles.

open_agricultural.notes: Best off-road mobility but lowest concealment.

coastal_ground_sensor.value: radar/EO horizon-limited; at 10 m sensor height radio horizon ~13 km to sea-skimming target plus target height

urban_ground_sensor.value: 50-300 m street-level; high-rise sensors can extend to km-scale corridors

wetland.radar_signature_reduction: moderate if reed/terrain masking; poor thermal cover in open bog

coastal.radar_signature_reduction: low on open shore, higher among islands/rocks/harbours

urban.radar_signature_reduction: high masking and multipath; UAV overhead still a major threat

declassified_public_intelligence_context.purpose: Presentation layer for a wake-up-call audience: it distinguishes what is officially public, what is declassified/formerly classified, what is OSINT, and what is intentionally not included.

bottom_line.statement: Publicly released data alone shows that modern Baltic and Indo-Pacific scenarios are dominated by kill-chain speed: detect, classify, decide, launch, assess, reload.

The most dangerous numbers are often not maximum ranges; they are readiness, stockpile depth, reload tempo, sensor discrimination, EW effectiveness, and command latency. Those are generally not public.
For public modelling, use wide uncertainty bands and scenario stressors rather than single-point classified-looking values.

allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_can_be_included: Publicly stated or widely assessed ranges, generic warhead classes, and launch-platform categories.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_stays_out: Current targeting procedures, mission data, route planning, real stockpile counts, timing windows, and active intelligence on defended locations.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.presentation_warning: Even conservative public ranges force defenders to plan for multi-axis standoff attack rather than only border-adjacent threats.

allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_can_be_included: Published launcher/radar family, nominal missile range bands, and generic layered-defense roles.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_stays_out: Actual emitter locations, radar modes, classified ECCM, operator tactics, kill probabilities, and live order of battle.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.presentation_warning: A single advertised range figure understates the problem; overlapping sensors, mobility, decoys, and EW create the real barrier.

allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_can_be_included: Public endurance, aircraft type, general radar/SIGINT role, and broad public detection-range claims.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_stays_out: Collection priorities, emitter libraries, intercept geometry, processing performance, and classified target sets.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.presentation_warning: AEW/SIGINT platforms do not need to shoot to be decisive; they compress the time between detection and engagement.

allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_can_be_included: Public system names, broad roles such as communications jamming or radar interference, and broad non-operational range claims where officially released.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_stays_out: Waveforms, bands, power levels, effectiveness probabilities, vulnerabilities, and real-world sensor degradation values.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.presentation_warning: EW should be modelled as uncertainty and delay, not as a magic on/off switch.

allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_can_be_included: Public range/endurance/speed/payload/cost estimates and role in reconnaissance-strike cycles.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.what_stays_out: Current launch sites, flight routes, target lists, countermeasure effectiveness, and live tactical lessons that enable evasion.
allowed_sensitive_but_public_examples.presentation_warning: Low-cost drones can force high-cost defensive decisions even when many are intercepted.

examples: current operational plans, tasking, routes, or locations
not_included_categories.handling: Do not include; use null fields and explanatory notes.
Do not include; use high-level doctrine and public capability envelopes only.
Do not include as precise values; use qualitative bands or scenario sliders.

wake_up_call_talking_points.headline: Public data is already enough to show the scale of the threat.
wake_up_call_talking_points.speaker_note: The absence of classified numbers should not be mistaken for safety. Public envelopes already show overlapping missile, sensor, EW, and drone layers.

wake_up_call_talking_points.headline: The missing data is usually the most important data.
wake_up_call_talking_points.speaker_note: Pk, stockpile depth, readiness, sensor discrimination, command latency, and crew fatigue decide outcomes but are not safe or credible to fill as exact public values.

wake_up_call_talking_points.headline: Model uncertainty explicitly.
wake_up_call_talking_points.speaker_note: For drawing-board simulations, include uncertainty bands, degraded comms, delayed detection, false tracks, and saturation instead of pretending exact numbers are known.

wake_up_call_talking_points.headline: Layered defense is a system-of-systems problem.
wake_up_call_talking_points.speaker_note: Aircraft, GBAD, naval sensors, EW, logistics, and civilian resilience should be briefed together; no single platform specification captures the fight.

recommended_simulation_controls.capability_values: Use public values as upper-bound or nominal envelopes, not guaranteed combat performance.
recommended_simulation_controls.pk: Represent as scenario slider with low/medium/high bands; default to null unless public test data is available and clearly contextualized.
recommended_simulation_controls.ew: Represent as probabilistic latency, track-quality degradation, false tracks, or communications delay; do not assign precise jamming probabilities.
recommended_simulation_controls.logistics: Use sensitivity analysis for ammunition and fuel rather than exact classified stockpiles.
recommended_simulation_controls.air_defense: Model layers, mobility, reload limits, decoys, and command latency; do not model live locations.

exact_probability_of_kill.note: Exact Pk depends on target profile, aspect, altitude, countermeasures, operator proficiency, weather, software, missile lot, and engagement geometry; generally not public.

exact_radar_range_against_stealth.note: Public values are not credible for exact stealth detection. Use qualitative detection/track/engage bands and uncertainty.

exact_ew_jam_probability.note: EW effectiveness is highly scenario-dependent and not safely represented as a public single-point probability.

current_order_of_battle_locations.note: Use notional locations or historical/public exercise data only; do not include current target-quality deployments.

classified_or_leaked_documents.note: Not included, even if found online. Use official declassified/public releases instead.
